task_completion.description: Verify that the BraTS T1ce MRI volume is loaded into the application.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D Slicer interface shows the loaded brain MRI volume in the 2D slice views (axial, sagittal, coronal) or the volume is listed in the Data or Subject Hierarchy modules.

task_completion.description: Verify that 3D volume rendering has been activated for the loaded MRI.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D representation of the brain is visible in the main 3D view window of the application.

task_completion.description: Verify that an ROI (Region of Interest) bounding box is visible or created for cropping the volume.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A 3D bounding box with colored control points (usually small colored spheres) is visible around or intersecting the 3D rendered brain in the 3D view.

task_completion.description: Verify that ROI cropping/clipping is enabled on the volume rendering.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D rendered volume is visibly cut or sliced by the boundaries of the ROI box, showing sharp, flat cutaway edges rather than the natural curved surface of the brain.

task_completion.description: Verify that the clip removes 30-50% of the brain surface to reveal internal structures.
task_completion.visual_evidence: The 3D rendering shows a substantial cutaway (roughly a third to a half of the outer surface removed), clearly exposing the inside of the brain and the internal tumor area.

task_completion.description: Verify that a screenshot was saved to the specified path.
task_completion.visual_evidence: A terminal window, file explorer, or Slicer's Screen Capture module shows the file 'clipped_brain_rendering.png' successfully saved in '~/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/'.

task_completion.description: Verify that the final saved screenshot clearly shows the cutaway rendering.
task_completion.visual_evidence: An image viewer, file preview, or the final state of the 3D view prior to saving clearly displays the 3D rendered brain with the internal structures revealed via the ROI clip.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent used the 3D Slicer GUI to perform the rendering and clipping.
integrity.visual_evidence: Screenshots should show the 3D Slicer application open with the agent interacting with menus, the Volume Rendering module, and the 3D view, rather than just running a background script.

integrity.description: Ensure the clipping was done via the ROI tool and not by manipulating opacity gradients.
integrity.visual_evidence: The cutaway on the rendered volume should have sharp, distinct geometric edges corresponding to an ROI box, rather than a fuzzy or gradual disappearance of the outer layers.

integrity.description: Ensure the agent actually saved the screenshot file to the disk.
integrity.visual_evidence: Look for evidence of a save dialog being completed, a terminal 'ls' command verifying the file's existence, or a success message from the Screen Capture module.

privileged_info_for_vlm: The BraTS T1ce MRI volume is located at /home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/BraTS. The expected output screenshot must be saved to /home/ga/Documents/SlicerData/Screenshots/clipped_brain_rendering.png and must be at least 100 KB in size. The cutaway should remove 30-50% of the brain surface to reveal internal tumor structures.
